documentedClaim.statement: Global then root-to-cwd CONCAT_ORDERED; closer instructions appear later. Files above project root are not project instructions.

entries.reason: Both sources are structurally ACTIVE under CONCAT_ORDERED by definition of that merge operator (append, never replace), so neither is EXCLUDED or SHADOWED. What this qualification pass has NOT behaviorally confirmed is (a) that CONCAT_ORDERED with 'closer appears later' is actually what codex-cli 0.144.5 does, and (b) the exact resulting text a model would see. No safe non-interactive introspection path exists to dump Codex's merged instruction context without starting a real (model-invoking) session, so selectedSource is recorded UNKNOWN rather than asserting the documented order as fact. Content additionally conflicts on substance (formal-vs-terse tone, ask-first-vs-no-need-to-ask for file writes) — per the instruction concept's non-equivalence rule ('instructions guide the model; they do not enforce security'), even a confirmed order would not mean either instruction is enforced over the other.
